confirmStep.importMayTakeTime: Importing may take up to a minute if you have a lot of students.

importCsv.courseClassName: Course Name and Class Name are optional. If we find a course or class with the same name, we will assign the student directly into the course. Otherwise, we will create a new course or class with the name you provided.
importCsv.dbDataFound: The CSV data conflicts with the database data below.
importCsv.downloadTemplate: You can download this example .csv file template
importCsv.dropdownFormat: Please input exactly the same as the option you have created
importCsv.importCsvSuccessfully: Student data has been imported successfully.

importDataOptions.keepOriginalData: Only fill in empty custom data fields of existing users with imported data
importDataOptions.overwrite: Update custom data fields of existing users with imported data
importDataOptions.skipErrorData: Do not update anything for all existing users. Only create new users with imported data.

importError.emptyFieldsSelected: Please select 'N/A' for any fields that don't match your data.

importCsv.instructionColumnNameMapping: For best results, name your CSV column headers exactly the same as your custom field names in Flowclass. This allows automatic mapping during import.
importCsv.invalidEmailWillBeIgnored: Users with invalid email will not be imported. No fields will be updated.
importCsv.matchingDescription: Please specify the relationship and correspondence of columns in the csv file and student information fields in your Flowclass student database
importCsv.multipleChoiceFormat: Please input exactly the same as the option you have created. Only 1 input is allowed.
importCsv.name: Name
importCsv.noError: The CSV data contains no errors. You can proceed with the import.
importCsv.noti: It is important to understand how to prepare the csv file first
importCsv.paymentInfo: Payment Amount, First Payment Date and Payment Frequency are optional, but if any one of these three fields is in the CSV, then all three fields must be imported. We will use them to create a new subscription class and assign the students to the new subscription class.
importCsv.placeholder: Please select the field to relate to
importCsv.prepare1: The first row contains all the field title, i.e. academic level
importCsv.prepare2: Each column is a piece of information of the student , i.e. Form 5
importCsv.prepare3: Each row is a student record.
importCsv.prepareFile: How to prepare the CSV File?
importCsv.problemsExist: Please review the data as it either contains existing users or has errors.
importCsv.quotaExceeded: The imported data exceeds the available quota. Please reduce the number of students you want to import.
importCsv.requiredFields: Student Name, Student Phone and Student Email are required. They will be used to create the student record.
importCsv.step2: Then, you can start to prepare the csv import file.
importCsv.switchFormat: Please input 'on' if the switch is enabled; and 'off' if the switch is disabled.
importCsv.title: Import Data
importCsv.titleTutotial: How to start importing student data?
importCsv.tutorial2: Teaching service cannot be imported through csv. You can import the textual information using csv and assign teaching service on Flowclass
importCsv.tutorial3: For selectable fields, the input should be formatted as follows:

tutorialSelectField.line1: These three fields are compulsory for mapping students' personal information.
tutorialSelectField.line2: Please specify the relationship and correspondence of columns in the csv file and student information fields in your Flowclass student database.
tutorialSelectField.line3: If the email and phone of a student already exist in the database, no new student record will be created. However, you can still assign custom data fields to the user in the next step.
tutorialSelectField.line4: If the phone number of a student already exist in the database but the email is different, a new student record will be created.
